title: Stretching the Word Ceasefire

mood.label: Heavy going
mood.summary: A world at 47/100 — the first real de-escalation signals in weeks as Israel and Hezbollah agree to halt hostilities and a US-Iran understanding hovers, yet strikes in Lebanon and Gaza go on, Sudan's war takes dozens at a wedding, and a record heat season holds; platypuses, dark-matter physics and steady markets keep a flicker of light.

sampleSources: Strudel default library — sampled piano, vibraphone (VCSL), RolandTR808
pattern: stack(s("bd ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~, ~ ~ ~ ~ rim ~ ~ ~, ~ ~ hh ~ ~ ~ [hh hh] ~").bank("RolandTR808").gain(0.44).room(0.26).hpf(110), note("a1 ~ ~ ~ d1 ~ ~ ~ f1 ~ ~ ~ e1 ~ ~ ~").s("sine").attack(0.02).release(0.7).lpf(400).gain(0.55), note("<[a2,c3,e3] [d3,f3,a3] [f2,a2,c3] [e3,g3,b3]>").s("piano").gain(0.52).room(0.5).rsize(5).pan(0.5), note("<[a3,c4,e4] [d4,f4,a4] [f3,a3,c4] [e4,g4,b4]>").s("sawtooth").attack(1.2).decay(0.4).sustain(0.6).release(3).lpf(sine.range(520,1600).slow(12)).gain(0.12).room(0.6).rsize(6), note("a4 ~ b4 c5 ~ b4 ~ ~ d5 ~ c5 a4 ~ b4 ~ ~ f4 ~ a4 c5 ~ b4 ~ ~ e4 ~ g4 a4 ~ b4 ~ ~").s("vibraphone").gain(0.34).room(0.5).pan(0.46).off(0.125, x => x.gain(0.14).pan(0.62).lpf(1800)), note("e5 ~ ~ ~ ~ c5 ~ ~ ~ ~ d5 ~ ~ ~ ~ ~").s("piano").gain(0.3).room(0.5).pan(0.58).delay(0.28).delaytime(0.4).jux(rev))

lines: A man stood up and said the guns agreed to learn to wait,
no fire on the southern suburbs, no answer from the dark.
At a table in Washington they spelled it twice, then twice again,
and somewhere over the Strait a tanker eased back through the arc.
But eight more names came in from Lebanon before the dawn,
and a wedding in Kutum is a number now, not a song.
Still a river in the south found platypus kits in the reeds,
and in a lab a buried signal made the dark a little less long.
Forty-seven, stretching the word ceasefire till it almost holds,
a little more light than yesterday, the same cold in the cold.
We write the truce and the toll on the same unfinished page —
still here, still counting, leaning on a hope that isn't safe.
